description: Bi-weekly digest covering ecosystem announcements, new crates, company adoption, and one highlighted open-source project. 2,000+ subscribers.
title: Rust Bytes

description: Community-edited weekly digest. Covers crates, blog posts, RFC updates, and job listings. Published every Tuesday without fail.
title: This Week in Rust

description: Long-running help forum. Thoughtful answers, minimal noise. Good place to ask design questions before opening a GitHub issue.
title: users.rust-lang.org

description: Official team updates — working group notes, compiler progress, and governance changes. Low frequency, high signal.
title: Inside Rust Blog

description: Language design pre-RFCs and working group discussions. Useful for tracking where the language is heading.
title: internals.rust-lang.org

description: High-volume community feed. Project announcements, learning resources, and release posts. Noisy but good for discovery.
title: r/rust

description: Interviews with library authors, core team members, and ecosystem contributors. Useful for understanding the people behind the crates you use.
title: Rustacean Station

description: Interviews with teams running Rust in production. Covers real-world tradeoffs, migration stories, and performance wins. Published by corrode.dev.
title: Rust in Production

description: Active chat room for embedded Rust development. Good for questions about HALs, bare-metal targets, and peripheral drivers.
title: Embedded Rust — Matrix

description: Regional Rust community connecting developers across India through meetups, discussions, and learning resources.
title: Rust India

description: Brazilian Rust community connecting local meetup groups across Belo Horizonte, Floripa, Rio, and São Paulo.
title: Rust Brasil

description: Structured Rust curriculum with explanations and hands-on exercises. Covers core language features from basics to async.
title: Rust Exercises

description: Four deep-dive essays by the author of serde, syn, anyhow, and thiserror. Covers async/await pitfalls, reference mental models, soundness bugs in safe code, and scaling triage for open source teams.
title: Essays by dtolnay

description: A list of Rust language mentors tagged by topics of interest.
title: awesome-rust-mentors

description: Repositório de empresas e projetos brasileiros que usam Rust.
title: eu-uso-rust

description: Русскоязычный сайт о языке программирования Rust
title: rustycrate.ru

description: leetrs is a blazing-fast, Rust-powered CLI and engine designed to make solving LeetCode problems from the terminal a first-class developer experience.
title: leetrs
